title: What is a Loop? 🔁
description: Don't repeat yourself! Make the computer do the hard work.

slides.title: The Definition of Insanity 🤪
Enter the Loop 🔁
slides.content:
## DRY: Don't Repeat Yourself

One of the most important rules in programming is **DRY** (Don't Repeat Yourself).

If you find yourself copy/pasting code, you should probably be using a **Loop**.

A loop is a structure that tells JavaScript: "Take this block of code, and run it over and over again until I tell you to stop."

quizData.question: What does DRY stand for in programming?
quizData.explanation: DRY stands for Don't Repeat Yourself. If you find yourself writing the exact same code twice, there is usually a more efficient way to do it!

slides.title: The 3 Types of Loops 🛡️
slides.content:
## The Loop Family

JavaScript gives us three main traditional ways to loop. We'll learn exactly how to use each one in the next lessons, but here is a quick preview:

1. **`for` Loop**: Used when you know EXACTLY how many times you want the code to run (e.g., "Run this 10 times").
2. **`while` Loop**: Used when you want code to run as long as a condition remains true, but you don't know when it will end (e.g., "Run this while the enemy is still alive").
3. **`do...while` Loop**: Exactly like a `while` loop, but guarantees the code will run at least ONCE before checking.

quizData.question: Which loop is best when you know exactly how many times you need to repeat something? (e.g., exactly 5 times)
quizData.explanation: The 'for' loop is specifically designed to run a block of code a known, specific number of times.
